seo_title: UK Visa Portal Leaked Thousands of Applicants' Passports and Selfies…
meta_description: Thousands of UK visa applicants' passports and selfies were leaked online due to a third-party website's security breach, but the company has not fixed the…
body_html:
Thousands of UK visa applicants' passports and selfies were leaked online due to a third-party website's security breach.

The third-party website exposed applicants' sensitive documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. The company sent attorneys instead of fixing the issue. No fix has been implemented yet.

Sensitive Information Applicants' passports and selfies were exposed online.

Company Response The company sent attorneys instead of fixing the issue.

Fix Status No fix has been implemented yet.

Applicants' sensitive documents were compromised. The incident may have caused stress and inconvenience to applicants. The company's response was inadequate.

Recommendations Companies handling sensitive information should prioritize security measures to prevent such incidents in the future.

source.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
source.summary: A third-party website used by the UK to process visa applications has leaked thousands of applicants' passports and selfies online, exposing their sensitive information. The leak was not addressed by the company, which instead dispatched attorneys to address the issue. This incident highlights a significant security breach in the UK's visa application process.
